---
title: "Parsing Function"
format: html
---
1. Write a function that parses the files and generate a table or report with metadata and key information from the structures, including PDB ID, protein name, source organism, determination method, authors, release date, resolution, and the number of peptide chains and other molecules.
Tip
You may find some amino acids that are not canonical. If so, you can either change to the canonical one or use X in the fasta sequence.


Para encontrar el API de RCSB PDB, he seguido este link: https://data.rcsb.org/redoc/index.html


he añadido otro link de:
- entry
- entity
(explicaría en el texto la diferencia de datos o de archivos de cada uno, ya que no vienen todos los datos en uno directamente)
He hecho este códgio de abajo basado en lo de Lucía de arriba:

aun así, para la entrega yo no pondría los códigos de dentro del diccionario de metadata así, sino que lo expandiría un poco más (igual extraer todo por separado con más pasos y luego meterlo en metadata?) para que quede algo más claro

```{python}
#| label: setup-libraries
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| 
import requests
import pandas as pd
```

DUDA: me pone en la web que puso lucía que las posibles salidas son directamente:
200 o 404, eso es que no puede haber más?

```{python}
#| label: extract_metadata_function
def extract_join_metadata(data_dict, metadata_key, subkey=None):
    """
    Extract values from a metadata dictionary and join them into a string.

    Parameters
    ----------
    data_dict : dict
        Dictionary containing the metadata entries.
    metadata_key : str
        Key in `data_dict` whose value should be a list.
    subkey : str, optional
        Key to use within each list element.  
        If `None` the list elements are used directly.

    Returns
    -------
    str
        Comma-separated string of extracted values.
        "N/A"` if the list is empty or no values were found.
    """
    # Retrieve the list of items associated with metadata_key
    items = data_dict.get(metadata_key, []) # default to an empty list if not found

    extracted_items = [] # empty list

    if subkey: # In case there is a subkey
        for item in items: # Iterate through items
            # Get the value for the specific subkey
            value = item.get(subkey) # NO second arg to avoid list of "N/A"
            if value: 
                # If there is a value, it is added to the list
                extracted_items.append(value)
   
    # Return a string (comma-separated) if data was found 
    if extracted_items:
        return ", ".join(extracted_items) 
    else: # Otherwise, return "N/A"
        return "N/A" 
```


```{python}
#| label: handle_API_error_function

def handle_API_error(id, status_code = None, flag_timeout = False):
    """
    Handles HTTP request errors or timeouts and prints informative messages in
    order to centralize the management of API request errors.

    Parameters
    ----------
    id : str
        PDB ID queried in the request.
    status_code : int
        HTTP status code returned by the request. 
        None by default (in case of timeout error).
    flag_timeout : bool
        Indicates whether the request failed due to a timeout.
        If True, the status_code is ignored.
        alse by default.

    Returns
    -------
        bool (True)
            True if the request was successful (status code 200).
            None returned for any error or timeout condition.
    """
    if flag_timeout: # If a timeout occurred, skip all other checks
        print(f"[408] Timeout error for ID: {id}.")
        
    else:
        if status_code == 200: # If the request was successful, returns True
            return True
        
        elif status_code == 301 or status_code == 302:
            # Resource has been permanently or temporarily moved
            print(f"[301] or [302] The object was moved to other route")

        elif status_code == 400:
            # Request processed but considered unsuccessful
            print(f"[400] Not valid ID: {id}")

        elif status_code == 404:
            # Request processed but no result was obtained
            print(f"[404] Result not found for ID: {id}")

        elif status_code == 429:
            # Request rejected due to request rate limits
            print(f"[429] Error: Too many requests.")

        elif status_code == 500 or status_code == 503:
            # Request failed due to internal server issues
            print("[500] or [503] Internal Server Error")

        else: # For any other error status code
            print(f"Error: {status_code}")
        
        return # For all cases except status_code == 200, function returns None
```

```{python}
#| label: get_metadata_function

import requests

def get_metadata_info(pdb_id):
    """
    Parse a PDB (Protein Data Bank) file and extract metadata information.
    Extracts and formats some metadata via extract_join_metadata function.
    Handles timeout exception adn 404 error during HTTP requests.
    
    Parameters
    ----------
    pdb_id : str
        Unique identifier for the PDB file.
    
    Returns
    -------
    metadata : dict
        Dictionary containing PDB metadata.
        Or None if an error occurs.
    """
    # Define endpoints for RCSB PDB Data API 
    # Entry API: Global metadata -> PDB ID; Determination Method; Authors; 
    #                               Release Date; Resolution; Number of peptide chains; 
    #                               Number of Other molecules
    url_entry = f"https://data.rcsb.org/rest/v1/core/entry/{pdb_id}"

    # Entity API: Specific data for cheminal entities
    entity_id = 1 # Using 1 for the primary polymer (protein)
    url_entity = f"https://data.rcsb.org/rest/v1/core/polymer_entity/{pdb_id}/{entity_id}" 

    # Handling with Timeout and Errors (only two possibilities: 200 / 404)
    try:
        response_entry = requests.get(url_entry,
                                     timeout = 100) 
        response_entity = requests.get(url_entity,
                                      timeout = 100) 

    except requests.exceptions.Timeout: # Handling Timeout Error
        handle_API_error(id, flag_timeout = True)
        return
    
    # Validate that PDB ID exists in both endopoints
    flag_entry = handle_API_error(pdb_id, status_code = response_entry.status_code)
    flag_entity = handle_API_error(pdb_id, status_code = response_entity.status_code)

    if not flag_entry or not flag_entity:
        return
    
    # Execution continues only if a 200 OK status is received
    
    # Parse JSON responses
    data_entry = response_entry.json()
    data_entity = response_entity.json()

    ############ Metadata extraction ############

    # ---- PDB ID
    id = data_entry.get("rcsb_id", "N/A") # Default to "N/A" if rcsb_id is not found

    # ---- Protein name
    # Default to empty dict to safely chain subsequent .get() calls
    polymer_entity = data_entity.get("rcsb_polymer_entity", {}) 
    prot_name = polymer_entity.get("pdbx_description", "N/A") 
    
    # ---- Source Organism
    # Extract and format via extract_join_metadata
    source_organisms = extract_join_metadata(data_entity,
                                            "rcsb_entity_source_organism", 
                                            subkey="ncbi_scientific_name") 

    # ---- Determination Method
    det_method = extract_join_metadata(data_entry, 
                                      "exptl", 
                                      subkey="method")

    # ---- Authors
    primary_cit = data_entry.get("rcsb_primary_citation", {}) 
    authors = extract_join_metadata(data_entry, 
                                   "rcsb_authors") 

    # ---- Release date
    acc_info = data_entry.get("rcsb_accession_info", {}) 
    date = acc_info.get("initial_release_date", "N/A")

    # ---- Resolution
    entry_info = data_entry.get("rcsb_entry_info", {}) 
    resolution = entry_info.get("resolution_combined", []) 
    if resolution: # If resolution is not an empty list
        resolution = resolution[0] # To remove "[]" 
    else:
        resolution = "N/A"

    # ---- Number of peptide chains
    num_chains = entry_info.get("polymer_entity_count_protein", "N/A")

    # ---- Other molecules
    molecules = entry_info.get("nonpolymer_entity_count", "N/A")

    ############ Metadata dictionary ############

    # Assemble the metadata dictionary
    metadata = {
        "PDB ID": id,
        "Protein Name": prot_name,
        "Source Organims": source_organisms,
        "Determination Method": det_method,
        "Authors": authors,
        "Release Date": date,
        "Resolution": resolution,
        "Number of Peptide Chains": num_chains,
        "Number of Other Molecules": molecules
    }
    return metadata
```



```{python}
#| label: metadata_to_csv_function

import pandas as pd
def save_metadata_csv(pdb_id, filename):
    """
    Extracts metadata from PDB ID via get_metadata_info function
    Stores the results in a pandas DataFrame and writes it to a CSV file.
    
    Parameters
    ----------
    pdb_id : str
        PDB ID to be parsed.
    filename : str
        The output CSV file path where metadata will be saved.
    
    Returns
    -------
    df : pd.DataFrame
        A pandas DataFrame containing the metadata.
    """
    # Fetch metadata dictionary for the single PDB ID
    metadata_info = get_metadata_info(pdb_id) 

    # Pass as a list to avoid "Scalar values" index error in Pandas
    df = pd.DataFrame([metadata_info])

    # Export the DataFrame to a CSV file
    df.to_csv(filename, index=False) # index=False to prevent writing row numbers
    
    print(f"Metadata for {pdb_id} successfully saved to {filename}")

    return df
```

2. Parse the data to extract the protein structural information from the PDB file in tabular format, then compute the pairwise distance matrix for each PDB structure. The distance between two amino acids is usually calculated as the 3D Euclidean distance between their C-alpha (CA) atoms.


```{python}
#| label: get_structural_info_function

def get_structural_info(pdb_id):
    """
    docstring
    """
    # Define endpoints for ****
    url = f"https://files.rcsb.org/download/{pdb_id}.pdb"
    
    try:
        response = requests.get(url, 
                               timeout = 100)
    
    except requests.exceptions.Timeout:
        handle_API_error(id, flag_timeout = True)
        return

    # Handle HTTP status code with handle_API_error and check if the request was successful
    Flag_species = handle_API_error(id, status_code = response_species.status_code)
    
    if not Flag_species:  # Proceed only if the request was successful (Flag_species: True)
        return
    if response.status_code == 404:
        print(f"[404] Result not found for ID: {pdb_id}")
        return
    ### creo que hay q añadir más handling errors en este caso
    # creo que sí puede haber más respuestas que 200 / 404 
    # (tenemos una función ya hecha para handling errors de apis de bases de datos biomed si eso la pego))
    aa_map = {
    'ALA': 'A', 'ARG': 'R', 'ASN': 'N', 'ASP': 'D', 'CYS': 'C', 'GLN': 'Q', 'GLU': 'E', 
    'GLY': 'G', 'HIS': 'H', 'ILE': 'I', 'LEU': 'L', 'LYS': 'K', 'MET': 'M', 'PHE': 'F', 
    'PRO': 'P', 'SER': 'S', 'THR': 'T', 'TRP': 'W', 'TYR': 'Y', 'VAL': 'V'
}
    #L: Me gusta mucho como está hecha la función, lo dejaría tal cual. Pero si es verdad que habría que comprobar lo de los errores para todas las llamadas a la API. 
    if response.status_code == 200:
        pdb_data = response.text #.text because is a .pdb file

        coords = [] # List to store coordinates
        for line in pdb_data.split("\n"): # Each line
            if line.startswith("ATOM"): # Processing only atom lines
                ##### WARNING -> Fixed-column parsing (not split/tab) is required by the PDB standard 
                ###(EN PDB NO SE SEPARA POR TABULADORES VAYA, son columnas fijas)
                atom_name = line[12:16].strip() #strip to delete blank spaces
                if atom_name == "CA": # If it is CA
                    # --- EXTRAER AMINOÁCIDO ---
                    aa_name_3 = line[17:20].strip() # Columna fija para el nombre del residuo
                    aa_name_1 = aa_map.get(res_name_3, "X") # 'X' si no es un AA estándar

                    coord_x = line[30:38].strip()
                    coord_y = line[38:46].strip()
                    coord_z = line[46:54].strip()

                    indiv_coords = {"x": float(coord_x),
                              "y": float(coord_y),
                              "z": float(coord_z)
                            }
                    chain = line[21:22].strip()
                    residue_number = line[22:26].strip()
                    
                    canonical_chain=CheckPROTEIN(chain)

                    coords.append({
                        "chain": canonical_chain,
                        "residue_number": residue_number,
                        "x": float(coord_x),
                        "y": float(coord_y),
                        "z": float(coord_z)
                        })
        # Transform the data into a df
        df_coords = pd.DataFrame(coords)
    return df_coords

# Try for every protein
pdb_list = ["1RBP", "1QRE", "1DGF"]
df_conjunto = {}
for protein in pdb_list:
    df_conjunto[protein] = get_structural_info(protein)

```

justificacion de porqué hay que acceder así:
El formato PDB es un estándar de columnas fijas (basado en las antiguas tarjetas perforadas). Esto significa que no importa cuántos espacios haya; lo que importa es la posición exacta (el índice) de cada carácter en la línea.

```{python check_canonical_aa}
def check_canonical_aa(sequence):
    '''
    This function checks if the sequence is compose by aminoacids
    Input: Sequence
    Output: Ambiguous nucleotides
    '''
    IsPROTEIN=False
    length=0
    NonCanonicalAA=[]
    while IsPROTEIN==False:
        for aminoacid in Protein_sequence:
            length+=1
            if aminoacid not in ['A', 'R', 'N', 'D', 'C', 'Q', 'E', 'G', 'H', 'I', 'L', 'K', 'M', 'F', 'P', 'S', 'T', 'W', 'Y', 'V']:

                NonCanonicalAA.append('X')
            else: 
                NonCanonicalAA.append(aminoacid)

            if length==len(Protein_sequence):
                IsPROTEIN=True
    CheckedProteinSequence=''.join(NonCanonicalAA)

    return CheckedProteinSequence
```


Generamos la matriz de distancias

```{python distance_matrix}
import numpy as np
import math

def distance_matrix(df):
    """
    docstring
    """
    size = N = len(df) # Matriz size = protein's length
    M = np.zeros((N,N)) # Create an empty matrix

    # Extract the xyz coordinates from the dataframe, they are a list of the xyz coordinates as a list for each CA [[x,y,z]]
    coordinates = df[["x","y","z"]].values 

    for row, coord1 in enumerate(coordinates): # xyz from reference CA (row)
        for column, coord2 in enumerate(coordinates): # xyz from the other CA (column)

            dx = coord2[0] - coord1[0]
            dy = coord2[1] - coord1[1]
            dz = coord2[2] - coord1[2]

            distance = math.sqrt(math.pow(dx,2) + math.pow(dy,2) + math.pow(dz,2))

            M[row,column] = distance # Fill the cell in matrix

    return M

for id,element in df_conjunto.items():
    print(id)
    print(distance_matrix(element))
```

3. Write a function for plotting the corresponding contact map for each structure, using a standard contact threshold (between 6-12 Å) and different colors for different contacts (intra- and interchain).

función que plotea
```{python plot_contact_matrix function}
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.patches as mpatches #to insert legend!
import numpy as np
def plot_contact_matrix(structural_info, distance_matrix, pdb_id="Protein"):
    """
    docstring
    """
    chain_per_res = structural_info["chain"].values

    # Initialize matrices with NaNs so they aren't plotted
    matrix_intra = np.full_like(distance_matrix, np.nan) #matrix about intrachain contacts
    matrix_inter = np.full_like(distance_matrix, np.nan)#matrix about interchain contacts

    ### adding the extraction of significant distances
    # using >=6 to avoid inmediate neighbours -> do not info about folding
    # using <=12 to exclude atoms that are too far  
    bool_matrix = (distance_matrix >= 6) & (distance_matrix <= 12)
        #####PONE EN EL ENUNCIADO ENTRE 6-12!


    # np.equal.outer genera una matriz donde [i,j] es True si chains[i] == chains[j]
    same_chain_bool_matrix = np.equal.outer(chain_per_res, chain_per_res) ###es un poco raro, opción B (en teoría es mazo más lenta, por si os gusta más:)

    #Lo dejaría de la forma que has puesto. No sabía que existía una función así, pero si es más rápido y eficiente la dejamos. A no ser que el profesor quiera que hasta esa parte la hagamos desde 0. 

    ###OPCIÓN B:
    # for i in range(len(distance_matrix)):        # Recorre las filas (residuo i)
    # for j in range(len(distance_matrix)):    # Recorre las columnas (residuo j)
    #     # Comparamos la cadena del residuo i con la del residuo j
    #     if chain_per_res[i] == chain_per_res[j]:
    #         same_chain_bool_matrix[i, j] = True
    #     else:
    #         same_chain_bool_matrix[i, j] = False

    # intrachain contacts:
    matrix_intra[bool_matrix & same_chain_bool_matrix] = distance_matrix[bool_matrix & same_chain_bool_matrix]# has to check two conditions: signficative distances (bool_matrix) and same chain (same_chain_bool_matrix)

    #interchain contacts:
    matrix_inter[bool_matrix & ~same_chain_bool_matrix] = distance_matrix[bool_matrix & ~same_chain_bool_matrix] # has to check two conditions: signficative distances (bool_matrix) and NOT same chain (~same_chain_bool_matrix)


    #### significative_distances is the matrix that we are going to plot:

    plt.figure(figsize=(8, 8))

    #cmap='binary' pone los valores con datos en negro y el fondo transparente/blanco
    #origin="lower" to invert y axis
    #PLOTTING INTRACHAIN MATRIX -> green
    plt.imshow(matrix_intra, cmap="Greens", interpolation="none", origin="lower")
    #PLOTTING INTRACHAIN MATRIX -> red
    plt.imshow(matrix_inter, cmap="Reds", interpolation="none", origin="lower")
    #####dato: Al haber NA, el segundo imshow no tapa al primero donde no hay datos
    

    #Legend
    #patches of color manually:

    blue_patch = mpatches.Patch(color="green", label="Intra-chain contact") #Mejor llamarlo green_patch no? O se llama blue por algo en específico?
    red_patch = mpatches.Patch(color="red", label="Inter-chain contact")
    patches = [blue_patch, red_patch]
    
    # Adding legend
    #bbox_to_anchor to add legend out of the plot
    plt.legend(handles=patches, loc="upper right", bbox_to_anchor=(1.35, 1))
 
    #Title and labels
    plt.title(f"CONTACT MAP (Protein: {pdb_id}) (6-12 Å)", fontsize=14)
    plt.xlabel("Residue Index", fontsize=12)
    plt.ylabel("Residue Index", fontsize=12)
    
    ####################### EXTRA (ya me decís que opinais)
    # Líneas divisorias de cadenas
    idx_changes = np.where(chain_per_res[:-1] != chain_per_res[1:])[0] #dice los indx donde cambia la cadena (va comparando quitando el último residuo (chain_per_res[:-1] y quitando el primero (chain_per_res[1:])))
    #OPCIÓN B POR SI LO VEIS MAL:
    # using the first row of the prev matrix generated
    # first_row = same_chain_bool_matrix[0]
    # idx_changes = []
    # for i in range(len(first_row) - 1):
    #     if first_row[i] != first_row[i+1]:
    #         idx_changes.append(i)
    
    for idx in idx_changes:
        ### adding 0.5: El residuo 0 ocupa el espacio que va desde -0.5 hasta 0.5. Su centro está en 0; luego así no tapamos el dato!
        plt.axhline(y=idx + 0.5, color="black", linestyle="--")
        plt.axvline(x=idx + 0.5, color='black', linestyle="--")

    plt.show()

#Lo hace todo

for id, element in df_conjunto.items():
    print(id)
    num_chains = element["chain"].nunique()
    print(f"Number of chains: {num_chains}")
    m_dist = distance_matrix(element) 
    plot_contact_matrix(element, m_dist, pdb_id=id) #### le metemos tmb element (del df_conjunto con la info de antes para que tenga información sobre la chain)
```

4. Try to interpret the plots. Can you identify any structure in the contact maps? If so, which ones? Determine the type of structure as well (monomer,homo- or hetero- dimer, tetramer… ).




# Prueba
pdb_list = ["1RBP", "1QRE", "1DGF"]
for id in pdb_list:
    to csv
